stimulus: The Proto-Indo-European language, reconstructed ancestor of hundreds of modern tongues spread across Europe and South Asia, ______blank like all reconstructed protolanguages, theoretical: linguists infer its features from systematic comparisons among descendant languages.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The singular verb "is" agrees in number with the singular subject "the Proto-Indo-European language."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "are" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject "the Proto-Indo-European language." Choice B is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject "the Proto-Indo-European language." Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "have been" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject "the Proto-Indo-European language."
